reason: Removing objective and pursuit conditions loses the criteria relative to which admissibility interpretation and resolution are assessed.
No non-circular total derivation of objective and pursuit conditions from the remaining primitives was demonstrated.
Investigation and Reasoning Calculus can vary independently; packaging objective conditions with rules is not a genuine merger.
No preregistered non-equivalent substitute with independent semantics was available.
Recovered context and objective machinery recreates the identity and operational role of Investigation.
Without explicitly distinguishable denoting objects the benchmark cannot preserve what is asserted transformed or recorded.
Objects become representations only under a denotation role; no non-circular derivation of that role was demonstrated.
Merging Representation with Representational Structure collapses element and organization identity conditions.
No non-equivalent substitute preserving denotation multiplicity and interpretation variance was preregistered.
Recovered tokens or nodes that denote process objects are commitment-equivalent to representations.
A collection without explicit organization does not preserve relations ordering dependencies or historical constraints.
Relations may be represented but no derivation identified which relations organize the collection without adding an organizing criterion.
Merging structure with representation cannot preserve reuse of the same representations across different organizations.
No non-equivalent organizational substitute was preregistered and executed.
Recovered adjacency ordering typing or containment relations recreate representational structure.
Syntax alone does not determine denotation; semantic and information preservation fail.
Meaning assignments can be stored but their semantic application was not derived without an interpretation-like mapping.
Merging Interpretation with Representation prevents one representation from receiving different meanings under different investigations.
No preregistered non-equivalent semantic mechanism was executed.
A recovered denotation or valuation map has the same identity and operational role as Interpretation.
An observed history does not determine which transitions are admissible or specify resolution procedures.
Observed transitions underdetermine governing rules; no total derivation of admissibility was shown.
Merging calculus with Investigation conflates objectives with rules and loses cases sharing an objective while using different calculi.
No non-equivalent substitute governing admissibility was preregistered and executed.
Recovered transition schemas and admissibility conditions recreate the Reasoning Calculus.
